message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #2 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #2 (CSS gaps) committed, pushed, and CI GREEN (2b5789a). 

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Changes: static/css/main.css — 7 items fixed:
1. .nav-logout — margin-inline-start: auto (right-aligned in header)
2. .nav-logout__button — styled as muted text link
3. .todo-add__group — spacing below input group
4. .todo-add__submit — fixed class name mismatch (was .todo-add__button)
5. .todo-add__error + :empty — danger color, small font, collapses when empty
6. .auth-form__message:empty — collapses empty error container
7. .auth-form__actions + .auth-form__cancel — flexbox layout, cancel as text-link

This addresses all of Steve Schoger's flagged CSS gaps.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good, the coordinator is collecting consensus. Let me provide my Driver consent for the record.

input.content:
I consent to Task #2. As Driver, a few design notes for the reviewers: **Design rationale:** - `.nav-logout` uses `margin-inline-start: auto` rather than wrapping in a `.site-header` because the template puts the logout form directly inside the `
`. The auto margin pushes it to the far right without needing to restructure the HTML. - `.nav-logout__button` mirrors the existing `.button--text` pattern already in the CSS (lines 660-675). I chose a separate class rather than adding `button--text` to the template because the BEM naming keeps it clear which component the button belongs to. - `.todo-add__error:empty` and `.auth-form__message:empty` both use `display: none` to collapse when empty. This works because Askama renders these elements with no whitespace when there is no error -- e.g. `

`. The `:empty` pseudo-class matches elements with no child nodes including text nodes, so this is safe as long as the template doesn't inject whitespace. I verified both templates render clean empty elements. - `.auth-form__actions` uses `align-items: center` so the Save button and Cancel link are vertically centered on the same row, even though they have different font sizes. - The `.todo-add__submit` rename fixes a real bug -- the old `.todo-add__button` class never matched anything in the template, so the submit button was getting no flex-shrink styling.
input.summary: I consent to Task #2, design rationale provided

routing.summary: I consent to Task #2, design rationale provided
routing.content:
I consent to Task #2. As Driver, a few design notes for the reviewers: **Design rationale:** - `.nav-logout` uses `margin-inline-start: auto` rather than wrapping in a `.site-header` because the template puts the logout form directly inside the `
`. The auto margin pushes it to the far right without needing to restructure the HTML. - `.nav-logout__button` mirrors the existing `.button--text` pattern already in the CSS (lines 660-675). I chose a separate class rather than adding `button--text` to the template because the BEM naming keeps it clear which component the button belongs to. - `.todo-add__error:empty` and `.auth-form__message:empty` both use `display: none` to collapse when empty. This works because Askama renders these elements with no whitespace when there is no error -- e.g. `

`. The `:empty` pseudo-class matches elements with no child nodes including text nodes, so this is safe as long as the template doesn't inject whitespace. I verified both templates render clean empty elements. - `.auth-form__actions` uses `align-items: center` so the Save button and Cancel link are vertically centered on the same row, even though they have different font sizes. - The `.todo-add__submit` rename fixes a real bug -- the old `.todo-add__button` class never matched anything in the template, so the submit button was getting no flex-shrink styling.

content.text: Both of my assigned tasks (#1 and #2) are complete. I've provided my consent and design rationale for Task #2. Waiting for the remaining reviewers to send their consent so we reach 9/9 consensus.
